ucs.title: [LLM] Gamaredon (Shuckworm/Aqua Blizzard) Office→mshta/wscript chain to dynamic-DNS or telegra.ph C2
ucs.description: Detects Gamaredon's signature initial-access chain: a weaponised Office document (often via remote-template injection) spawning mshta.exe/wscript.exe/cscript.exe/powershell.exe with a command line that references one of the dynamic-DNS providers Gamaredon repeatedly burns (.ddns.net, .hopto.org, .myftp.org, .sytes.net, .my.to, .myq-see.com), the .ru TLD, or telegra.ph dead-drop resolvers used by GammaLoad/PteroPSLoad/GammaSteel.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","powerpnt.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe") AND (Processes.process="*ddns.net*" OR Processes.process="*hopto.org*" OR Processes.process="*myftp.org*" OR Processes.process="*sytes.net*" OR Processes.process="*serveftp.com*" OR Processes.process="*my.to*" OR Processes.process="*myq-see.com*" OR Processes.process="*telegra.ph*" OR Processes.process="*telegraph.com*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process="*.sfx*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let GamaredonDdns = dynamic([".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".myftp.org",".sytes.net",".serveftp.com",".my.to",".myq-see.com","telegra.ph","telegraph.com"]);
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","powerpnt.exe"]);
let ChildBins = dynamic(["mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
| where FileName in~ (ChildBins)
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend HasDdns   = Cmd has_any (GamaredonDdns)
| extend HasRuTld  = Cmd matches regex @"https?://[a-z0-9\.\-]+\.ru[/\s\"\']"
| extend HasHta    = Cmd has_any (".hta", "mshta", ".sfx")
| where HasDdns or HasRuTld or HasHta
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          HasDdns, HasRuTld, HasHta, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let GamaredonDdns = dynamic([".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".myftp.org",".sytes.net",".serveftp.com",".my.to",".myq-see.com","telegra.ph","telegraph.com"]);
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","powerpnt.exe"]);
let ChildBins = dynamic(["mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
| extend NewLeaf    = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    '\\')[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (OfficeApps)
| where NewLeaf    in (ChildBins)
| extend Cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| extend HasDdns  = Cmd has_any (GamaredonDdns)
| extend HasRuTld = Cmd matches regex @"https?://[a-z0-9\.\-]+\.ru[/\s\"\']"
| extend HasHta   = Cmd has_any (".hta", "mshta", ".sfx")
| where HasDdns or HasRuTld or HasHta
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          HasDdns, HasRuTld, HasHta
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Gamaredon Office-Document mshta/wscript Chain to Dynamic-DNS or Telegra.ph C2
id: 8a3f5e6d-4b1c-4e1a-9c2f-gam0047uc01
status: experimental
description: Detects Gamaredon's signature delivery chain - Office app spawning a Windows scripting LOLBin with a command line referencing dynamic-DNS providers, .ru TLD, or telegra.ph dead-drop resolvers used by GammaLoad/PteroPSLoad.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0047/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1221
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.g0047
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  selection_indicator:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '.ddns.net'
      - '.hopto.org'
      - '.myftp.org'
      - '.sytes.net'
      - '.serveftp.com'
      - '.my.to'
      - '.myq-see.com'
      - 'telegra.ph'
      - '.hta'
      - '.sfx'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_indicator
falsepositives:
  - Rare legitimate macro that fetches templates from dynamic-DNS - whitelist after triage
level: high
ucs.rationale: Gamaredon (FSB Center 18, Crimea) is the most prolific Russian APT against Ukraine and burns through dynamic-DNS infrastructure faster than any other state actor - .ddns.net/.hopto.org/.myftp.org/.sytes.net are all repeatedly attributed to them by ESET, CERT-UA, Symantec/Shuckworm and Microsoft/Aqua Blizzard reporting. The combination of Office-app parent + Windows scripting LOLBin child + one of

_field_issues.message: `HasHta` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `HasHta` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Gamaredon PteroLNK USB self-propagation - mass .lnk creation in removable-drive root
ucs.description: Detects Gamaredon's PteroLNK USB worming module: a non-explorer process creating multiple .lnk files in the root of a removable drive (D:-Z:), which is how Gamaredon shadows real folders with malicious shortcuts that re-execute their VBScript loader on every USB transit. Self-propagating USB tradecraft is one of the most distinctive markers of this actor versus other Russian APTs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_names dc(Filesystem.file_name) as distinct_lnk from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" AND (Filesystem.file_path="D:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="E:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="F:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="G:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="H:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="I:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="J:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="K:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="L:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="M:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="N:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="O:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="P:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="Q:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="R:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="S:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="T:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="U:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="V:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="W:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="X:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="Y:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="Z:\\*") AND Filesystem.process_name!="explorer.exe" AND Filesystem.process_name!="msiexec.exe" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where distinct_lnk >= 3
ucs.kql:
let SuppressProcs = dynamic(["explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","wininit.exe"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FileName endswith ".lnk"
| where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\[^\\]*\.lnk$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (SuppressProcs)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| summarize LnkCount = count(),
            DistinctNames = dcount(FileName),
            SampleNames = make_set(FileName, 15),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DriveLetter = any(substring(FolderPath, 0, 2)),
            ParentProcCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
            by DeviceName,
               InitiatingProcessFileName,
               InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
               InitiatingProcessAccountName,
               bin(Timestamp, 10m)
| where DistinctNames >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let SuppressProcs = dynamic(["explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","wininit.exe"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".lnk"
| where TargetFilePath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\[^\\]*\.lnk$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf !in (SuppressProcs)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| summarize LnkCount = count(),
            DistinctNames = dcount(TargetFileName),
            SampleNames = make_set(TargetFileName, 15),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DriveLetter = any(substring(TargetFilePath, 0, 2)),
            ParentCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, ActorUsername, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where DistinctNames >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Gamaredon PteroLNK USB Worming - Mass .lnk Drop in Removable-Drive Root
id: 7c2e9f4a-1d3b-4e7c-b8a1-gam0047uc02
status: experimental
description: Detects Gamaredon's PteroLNK USB self-propagation module dropping multiple .lnk shortcut files in the root of a removable drive (D:-Z:) by a non-Explorer process. The .lnk files are used to shadow real folders and re-launch the VBScript stager whenever the USB is plugged in.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0047/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2024/01/19/operation-texonto-information-operation-disinformation-targeting-ukraine/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.lateral_movement
  - attack.t1091
  - attack.g0047
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_lnk:
    TargetFilename|re: '(?i)^[D-Z]:\\[^\\]*\.lnk$'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\installer.exe'
      - '\wininit.exe'
  condition: selection_lnk and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Backup or sync utilities (rclone, FreeFileSync) writing shortcut farms to removable media
  - Portable-app launchers that legitimately seed .lnk files
level: high
ucs.rationale: USB self-propagation via .lnk shadowing is a defining Gamaredon/Shuckworm tradecraft - documented by ESET (PteroLNK), Symantec, Microsoft (Aqua Blizzard) and CERT-UA. Almost no other state-grade APT still relies on USB worming as a primary lateral-movement vector, so a host suddenly producing a fan-out of .lnk files in a USB root - by something other than Explorer/installers - is a near-pathognomo
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
